https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hugh_de_Beauchamp_(sheriff)

Hugh de Beauchamp (sometimes Hugh of Beauchamp;[1] died after 1101) was a Norman who held lands in England after the Norman Conquest.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh de Beauchamp於大約1040年出生在法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯時，他的父親Robert de Beauchamp21歲，母親Adeliuza Alix de Meulan19歲。他於1070年之前與Matilda Taillebois結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1087年住在英國貝德福爾。他於1066年登記服兵役。他去世於大約1114年在英國英格蘭貝德福爾貝德福德享年76歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh de Beauchamp was born about 1040, in Calvados, Normandy, France, his father, Robert de Beauchamp, was 21 and his mother, Adeliuza Alix de Meulan, was 19. He married Matilda Taillebois before 1070. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He lived in Bedfordshire, England in 1087. He registered for military service in 1066. He died about 1114, in Bedford, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 76.

Il a épousé Matilda Taillebois avant 1070. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il a vécu à Bedfordshire, Angleterre en 1087. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1066. Il est décédé environ 1114, à Bedford, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 76 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh de Beauchampが約1040年にフランスノーマンディカルヴァドスで生まれた時，父Robert de Beauchampは21歳，母Adeliuza Alix de Meulanは19歳であった。1070年以前にMatilda Tailleboisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1087年にはイギリスベドフォードシュアイアに住んでいた。彼は1066年に兵役登録をした。彼は約1114年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアベドフォードで76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh de Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1040년 프랑스 노먼디 캘바도스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Beauchamp(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Adeliuza Alix de Meulan(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1070년 이전 Matilda Taillebois(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1087년도에 영국 베드퍼드실르에서 살았다. 그는 1066년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 대략 1114년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 베드퍼드에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh de Beauchamp родился около 1040 г. в местности калвадос, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Robert de Beauchamp было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Adeliuza Alix de Meulan, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda Taillebois перед 1070 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности бедфордшир, Англия в 1087 году. В 1066 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер около 1114 г. в местности бедфорд, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 76 лет.
